---
title: "Allometric Volume Equation Rebuild"
format: html
execute:
  eval: false
---

## Purpose

Walk through the INFYS/SNIF volume-equation rebuild and inspect the committed
schema artifacts copied into this tutorial repo.

Source scripts:

- `../mia-allometric/R/01_ingest_volume.R`
- `../mia-allometric/R/02_build_sqlite_volume.R`
- `../mia-allometric/R/03_export_parquet_volume.R`

The source paths assume `mia-tutorials` and `mia-allometric` are sibling
repositories. Replace `../mia-allometric` with your local source-repo path if
your checkout differs.

```{r}
library(here)

schema_path <- here::here("data", "mia_allometric", "schema_volume.sql")
schema <- readLines(schema_path, warn = FALSE)
cat(paste(head(schema, 60), collapse = "\n"))
```

## Pipeline stages

1. `01_ingest_volume.R` reconstructs workbook headers, joins lookup sheets,
   normalizes equation text, and parses applicability ranges.
2. `02_build_sqlite_volume.R` builds the relational volume SQLite database.
3. `03_export_parquet_volume.R` exports volume parquet artifacts.

```{bash}
cd <path-to-mia-allometric>
Rscript R/01_ingest_volume.R
Rscript R/02_build_sqlite_volume.R
Rscript R/03_export_parquet_volume.R
```

## Review questions

- Are `umafor_code` values preserved as strings?
- Are DBH and height ranges parsed into numeric min/max fields?
- Does every evaluable row have `equation_numpy`?
